meaning: n.闯入者,(为图私利)干涉他人事务者,无执照营业者
examples: The scientists' description of the interloper is certainly consistent with the bloom shown in this image.
She felt like an interloper in her own family.
The interloper, though, has never been found—until now.
Once considered an interloper with dubious endurance, Djokovic, 24, now stands as the sport's alpha.
This second pathway results in the production of further lymphocytes which attack the interloper.
